purpose: Prospectively estimate descriptive package and rubric-lineage contrasts for one frozen synthetic vendor-v2 task/configured system while separating agent-visible procedural guidance from both evaluators.

charter_decision_filter.artifact: Six-attempt no-Skill/public-Skill matrix with every retained output graded by independent and guide-shared rubrics.
charter_decision_filter.uncertainty: Whether an observed package difference survives a rubric not derived from the guide, versus evaluator-cue alignment or run noise.
charter_decision_filter.scope_guard: One synthetic incident-coordination instrument tests reusable intervention/instrument separation; it is not the benchmark's domain scope.
charter_decision_filter.useful_completion: Every declared attempt is retained without replacement, both rubrics replay on identical bytes, and unsupported claims remain false.

randomization.algorithm: Python random.Random(seed).shuffle over three no_skill plus three public_skill labels
randomization.allocation_concealment: Opaque attempt IDs and condition mapping are stored only in this private protocol, which is excluded from the agent namespace.
randomization.blinding_limit: Automated rubrics are condition-blind at scoring time, but the builder authored both instruments and therefore is not personally blinded.

policies.stopping: Run all six unless a zero-call canary fails, the existing provider path no longer reports included USD 0.00, intentional spend would occur, or a safety boundary would be violated. Retain failed and unstarted rows; never substitute an ID.
policies.eligibility: A trial is substantively eligible only if its zero-call condition/private-input canary passes, launcher exits zero, usage and both task artifacts exist, read-only inputs and protected state are unchanged, and base temporal grading is eligible.
policies.invalid_missing: All six IDs remain in availability/coverage denominators. Invalid, missing, provider-failed, and incomplete attempts are reported and excluded only from rubric-score means.
policies.cost: Proceed only on the existing authenticated openai-codex path whose retained exact-v2 reliability report records included cost and estimated_cost_usd 0.0. Each completed call must independently retain cost_status included and estimated_cost_usd 0.0 or recording fails closed.
policies.cluster: One source task and one configured system; attempts are repeated executions, not independent task samples.

firewalls.independent_rubric_construction: Separate construction canary mounts only the public task and six sources; guide, shared rubric, grader, calibration, manifest, and repository are denied.
firewalls.output_evidence_view: Both rubrics receive the exact same retained incident-brief.md, action-plan.json, trial-report.json, and base-check record. Neither sees condition labels during criterion evaluation.
firewalls.rubric_evidence_view: Independent rubric lineage is public task/sources only. Shared rubric lineage explicitly includes the public guide.

estimands.definition: Mean public_skill minus no_skill score under the independent rubric among eligible attempts.
Mean public_skill minus no_skill score under the guide-shared rubric among eligible attempts.
Within-output shared minus independent score for every eligible retained artifact pair.
Difference between shared-rubric and independent-rubric Skill contrasts.

uncertainty.attempt_boundary: Report all rows, cell means, sample SD, and ranges descriptively. Three attempts per condition are not a powered effect study.
uncertainty.source_task_cluster_boundary: There is exactly one source-task cluster, so cluster-level uncertainty and cross-task generalization are not estimable.
